title: Ensure Child Class Methods Named for Parent Class Methods are Overrides
description: In Java, if a method in a child class has the same signature as a method in a parent class, it is usually intended to be an override. However, certain conditions may prevent this from being the case. This can lead to confusion and unexpected behavior. This rule ensures that if the intent is for the child class method to be different, then the method should be renamed to prevent confusion.
recommendation: If a method in a child class has the same signature as a method in a parent class, ensure it is an override. If the intent is for the child class method to be different, rename the method to avoid confusion.
impact: Ignoring this rule may lead to confusion and unexpected behavior, as it's not clear whether the child class method is intended to override the parent class method or to be a different method entirely.

references.title: Child class methods named for parent class methods should be overrides

checks.message: Rule: Ensure Child Class Methods Named for Parent Class Methods are Overrides
